title: Signature verification with key-rotation retry on first failure
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:829-851

FINDING: After a signature verification failure, the code invalidates the actor cache entry and immediately re-fetches the actor document to get a potentially-rotated key, then retries verification. This correctly handles the key rotation scenario (remote server changes keys; our cached copy is stale) without permanently rejecting valid posts.

The single retry is the right limit — more retries would enable a DoS where a malicious actor can force repeated fetches by using a mismatched key. One retry per request is bounded and correct. Should be preserved if someone proposes removing the retry to 'simplify' error handling.
close_reason: Acknowledged: key rotation retry design is correct
